summary: Iteration 37 - Bloco B (Planos de Inspeção replacing templates). BACKEND 15/15 PASS (CRUD + resolver + migrar + categorias-disponiveis + RBAC + audit). FRONTEND CRITICAL FAILURE: ModalNovaInspecao throws ReferenceError 'templates is not defined' on render (App.js lines 1438 and 1460 still reference the removed `templates` state). The component is completely broken — page shows React 'Uncaught runtime errors' overlay and the modal never renders. Users cannot create new inspections at all.

pass_fail_matrix.FRONTEND ModalNovaInspecao renders witho: FAIL — ReferenceError: templates is not defined

minor.issue: Uses find_one for plano_tipo and plano_ativo — if multiple plans exist with the same tipo_equipamento+categoria (or same ativo_id+categoria), only the first match is merged; the others are silently ignored. Recommend using find().to_list() and merging all matches, OR enforce uniqueness via index.

Idempotent for DEFAULT_CHECKLISTS only. The legacy inspection_templates loop (line 887-910) does NOT check for an existing migrated copy — running migrar twice will duplicate any legacy templates. Add an existing-check by tipo_equipamento+categoria+nome before insert_one.

ui_bugs.issue: References undefined variable `templates` at line 1438 (`templates[tipoTab]?.nome || tipoTab`) and line 1460 (`templates[tipoTab]?.nome || ''`). Throws ReferenceError on render → React error overlay → modal never appears → users cannot create new inspections. Fix: replace `templates[tipoTab]?.nome` with `resolvedPlan?.categoria || tipoTab` or with a static category label map.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot test — modal blocked by ReferenceError. Resolver API call (loadPlan) is wired correctly in code but unreachable.

action_items: CRITICAL: App.js lines 1438 & 1460 — remove `templates[tipoTab]?.nome` references. The `templates` state was removed when migrating to the resolver-based flow but two leftover references remained. Suggested replacement: line 1438 → `Checklist — {tipoTab} ({checklist.length} itens)` or use a CATEGORIA_LABELS={mecanica:'Mecânica',eletrica:'Elétrica',lubrificacao:'Lubrificação'} map. Line 1460 → `Criar Inspeção ${CATEGORIA_LABELS[tipoTab]||''}`.
Improve resolver to merge ALL plans matching tipo_equipamento+categoria (and ativo_id+categoria) instead of find_one — current behavior silently drops additional plans.
Add idempotency to legacy inspection_templates migration (existing-check) so /api/planos-inspecao/migrar can be safely re-run.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js:1438,1460 — references to undefined `templates` (REGRESSION introduced when removing template selection UI). Component crashes on mount.

App.js:1273-1466 — ModalNovaInspecao does NOT contain the word 'Template' or 'Selecionar Template' in any visible text — Bloco B intent is preserved in the code; only the unresolved variable name remains.
App.js:1296 — resolver call uses correct params (ativo_id, categoria) ✓
App.js:1298-1300 — checklist mapping preserves all pergunta fields and seeds conforme/resultado/observacao as null ✓
App.js:1428-1434 — Plan info banner uses resolvedPlan.total_perguntas + plano_tipo/plano_ativo badges (good).
server.py:740 — sort by categoria ascending. Consider also sorting by created_at desc as secondary so newest first within a category.

server.py:838 — perguntas.sort by ordem. But when merging L1+L2, both may use overlapping ordem values (e.g. both start at 0); they get interleaved. Consider preserving L1-then-L2 by offsetting L2 ordem or adding a stable secondary key (origem).
server.py:854 — check_write_permission for migrar requires 'admin' only (PCM cannot migrate). Confirm this is intentional; spec says PCM creates/edits plans but migration is admin-only.

server.py:801 — resolver does not propagate organization scoping into the ativo lookup ({'id': ativo_id, 'deleted_at': None}). Cross-org ativo IDs could leak. Add 'organization_id': org_id to the ativo find.
seed_data_creation: Tests create+delete TEST_iter37_* plans inside /api/planos-inspecao. Cleanup performed in TestResolver.test_cleanup. TestAuditLog leaves one soft-deleted plano_inspecao with name 'TEST_iter37 audit check'. No production data modified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is solid — re-run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://procure-manutrix.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration37_planos_inspecao.py -v. After main agent fixes the `templates` ReferenceError in App.js (lines 1438 & 1460), retest frontend: navigate to /inspecoes, click 'Nova Inspeção', verify (a) modal opens without console error, (b) tabs tab-mecanica/tab-eletrica/tab-lubrificacao exist, (c) modal text does NOT contain 'Template' or 'Selecionar Template', (d) selecting an ativo from the combobox auto-populates checklist via /api/planos-inspecao/resolver, (e) plan info badge shows 'N perguntas carregadas' + 'Plano: {tipo_equipamento}' + '+ Específico {ativo_tag}'.
rca of the issue: Root cause (frontend): During the Bloco B refactor, the ModalNovaInspecao component was rewritten to use the new /api/planos-inspecao/resolver endpoint and the local `templates` state was removed. However, TWO render-time references to `templates[tipoTab]?.nome` were left behind: (1) App.js line 1438 inside the conditional checklist banner — `Checklist — {templates[tipoTab]?.nome || tipoTab} ({checklist.length} itens)`, and (2) App.js line 1460 inside the submit button label — `Criar Inspeção ${templates[tipoTab]?.nome || ''}`. Since `templates` is not declared in the component scope (and is not a global), JavaScript throws ReferenceError when React evaluates this JSX during render. The error is thrown immediately when the Inspecoes page mounts (because the modal element is in the tree, even if `isOpen=false` it depends on the Modal implementation), producing a full-page React DevServer error overlay. Reproduction: 1) Login as admin@manutrix.com / admin123. 2) Navigate to /inspecoes. 3) Either the page itself crashes with the overlay, or click 'Nova Inspeção' to trigger. Mitigation: Replace both `templates[tipoTab]?.nome` references with a static label map e.g. `const CAT_LABEL = {mecanica:'Mecânica', eletrica:'Elétrica', lubrificacao:'Lubrificação'}` and use `CAT_LABEL[tipoTab]`. Backend has no issue.
